original_text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles.
identified_property: policy implementation quality and supporting infrastructure

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, but the policy lacks any enforcement mechanism, includes no funding for charging infrastructure, and is undermined by conflicting local regulations and no coordinated grid planning.

variations.label: low
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, with only minimal funding allocated for sparse public charging stations, weak enforcement provisions, and no meaningful plan to upgrade the electrical grid to handle increased demand.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, accompanied by modest charging infrastructure expansion along major highways, basic consumer rebates, but lacking comprehensive grid modernization or robust dealer compliance oversight.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, supported by a statewide charging network expansion plan, meaningful consumer purchase incentives, clear dealer compliance timelines, and initial investments in grid capacity upgrades.

variations.label: high
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, backed by substantial public-private investment in a dense statewide charging network, significant grid modernization, robust consumer subsidies, strong enforcement with dealer accountability, and targeted support for rural and underserved communities.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: California mandates all new cars sold after 2035 must be electric or zero-emission vehicles, supported by a fully funded, world-class charging infrastructure in every community, comprehensive grid modernization with renewable energy integration, generous and equitable consumer incentives, extensive workforce retraining programs, rigorous enforcement, and a coordinated multi-agency transition plan ensuring affordability and accessibility for all residents.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier maintains the core California 2035 EV mandate while progressively improving the quality of policy implementation and supporting infrastructure—from actively undermined and unfunded (tier 1) through increasingly robust infrastructure investment, enforcement, equity provisions, and coordination (tiers 5–7). The distinctions are qualitative, not quantitative, focusing on comprehensiveness and quality of planning, funding, enforcement, and equity.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a well-implemented policy with world-class infrastructure, equitable access, grid readiness, workforce support, and rigorous enforcement maximizes the likelihood of a successful, fair, and smooth transition to zero-emission vehicles—achieving the policy's environmental goals while minimizing disruption and inequity.
